description: Returning early from a kernel error path without freeing allocations or dropping references, leaking memory and refcounts over uptime.
body: Failure mode: a function allocates memory or takes a reference, then hits an error and returns without unwinding, so each failure leaks; over uptime the system slowly exhausts memory or pins objects that can never be freed. Detection signals: kmemleak reports growing leaks; free memory declines under repeated failure injection; an error return path lacks the matching kfree/put. Do not return from an error path without cleanup. Safer fallback: use goto-based ordered unwind labels so each error path frees exactly what was acquired, audit every early return, and verify with kmemleak under allocation fault injection.
